results.name: 05_conflicting_requirements
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: The model defines a small physical system with requirements on external shape, enclosure material, and total mass; the intended stakeholder or end use is not stated.
- Operational domain: Not specified in the model text; appears to be a generic physical product/mechanical enclosure context.
- Model scope: Very limited requirements fragment containing four requirement definitions only.
- Known scope limitations: No structural decomposition, no quantitative material properties, no behavior, no interfaces, no allocations, no safety constraints, and no requirement relationships (`satisfy`, `refine`, derivation, containment) are present. Checks depending on those elements are assessed as not evidenced in this fragment and not raised as false conflicts.

CHECK PASSED: 1. Numeric Constraint Conflicts - The model contains only one explicit numeric bound (`side length no greater than 0.05 m`) and one system mass limit (`no more than 1 kg`); no second bound, subcomponent budgets, timing, throughput, power, or temperature constraints are present to create a demonstrable numeric contradiction within this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Behavioural Conflicts - No state machines, activities, transitions, triggers, guards, liveness conditions, or execution deadlines are present in the model fragment, so no behavioural conflict can be evidenced.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Interface & Protocol Conflicts - No ports, flows, connections, protocols, rates, directions, or handshake specifications are present in the model fragment, so no interface/protocol conflict can be evidenced.

CHECK PASSED: 4. Requirement-Level Conflicts - The stated requirements are potentially co-satisfiable as written, and `TradeResolution` explicitly asserts they are to be satisfied together with a precedence statement rather than introducing a contradiction. No explicit opposing quality attribute requirement is stated in the text.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Allocation Conflicts - No functions, physical components, processors, or allocation relationships are present in the model fragment, so no allocation conflict can be evidenced.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Safety vs. Functional Conflicts - No safety requirements, prohibited behaviors, watchdog constraints, or functional behavior requirements are present in the model fragment, so no safety-functional conflict can be evidenced.

CHECK PASSED: 7. Priority & Resolution - For the only potential trade tension in the fragment (shape/material/mass), `TradeResolution` provides an explicit resolution statement and priority ordering: mass limit and enclosure material take precedence, with internal volume allocation to be adjusted within the cubic size constraint.

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| None | 1.0 | HIGH | No conflicting requirements or constraints are explicitly evidenced in this limited model fragment. |

Overall Assessment Score: 1.0
Model Status: Good - minor improvements only
